answers: Flutter is Google's UI toolkit for building natively compiled apps for mobile, web, and desktop from a single codebase. Uses Dart language.
Flutter basics: learn Dart first (easy if you know Java/JS), then widgets, state management (Provider/Riverpod), navigation, and API integration.
Flutter vs React Native: Flutter has better performance and consistent UI across platforms. React Native has larger community and uses JavaScript. Both are great!
Learn Flutter: official docs (flutter.dev), Flutter Apprentice book, and YouTube channels like The Net Ninja. Build a simple app as your first project.
